checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?

evidence.location: 10-Q balance sheet, FY2013 vs FY2009 comparison

checklist.question: Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.location: Balance sheet PP&E net, FY2013 vs FY2009
Cash flow statement, capex vs D&A FY2009-FY2013

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?

evidence.location: Income statement / cash flow statement FY2012-FY2013

checklist.question: Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?
Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss=46,133,000 (Q1 2013); NetIncomeLoss=42,004,000 (Q2 2013); NetIncomeLoss=27,622,000 (Q3 2013); implied Q4 2013 NetIncomeLoss≈12,479,000 (FY2013 total 128,238,000 less prior quarters)

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.location: Balance sheet total liabilities FY2009 vs FY2013

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: 10-K/A filing_date=2012-04-20 (accession 0001193125-12-173208), filed over 14 months after original 10-K filing_date=2011-02-25 for the same FY2010/FY2009 periods, and containing full income-statement and balance-sheet line items (e.g., CostOfRevenue=763,958,000 FY2009, Liabilities=1,334,532,000 2010-12-31)

Repeated SEC correspondence/UPLOAD filings in 2010 (UPLOAD 2010-06-01, CORRESP 2010-06-15), 2011 (UPLOAD 2011-02-16, 2011-03-10), 2012 (UPLOAD 2012-04-11, CORRESP 2012-04-20), 2013 (UPLOAD/CORRESP May-October 2013), and 2014 (UPLOAD 2014-05-06, CORRESP 2014-05-09)

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Quarterly and annual data provided continuously from FY2008 (e.g., OperatingIncomeLoss=274,388,000 FY2008) through Q1 2014 (Revenues=833,746,000 Q1 2014), covering roughly 6 years of income statement, balance sheet and cash flow concepts

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Capital expenditures for store/rental merchandise fixtures grew far faster (PaymentsToAcquirePropertyPlantAndEquipment +57% FY2009→FY2013) than depreciation expense (+16% over same period) and far faster than revenue (+12.8%), while net PP&E nearly doubled its growth rate relative to revenue (PropertyPlantAndEquipmentNet +64.6% vs Revenues +12.8%, 2009-12-31 to 2013-12-31). This divergence is consistent with capitalization of costs that could otherwise be expensed, or extended useful-life assumptions, potentially understating depreciation/cost of revenue and overstating operating income in the near term.

rationale_evidence.quote: PropertyPlantAndEquipmentNet=204,551,000 (2009-12-31) vs 336,498,000 (2013-12-31); PaymentsToAcquirePropertyPlantAndEquipment=68,841,000 (FY2009) vs 108,367,000 (FY2013); Depreciation=65,788,000 (FY2009) vs 76,451,000 (FY2013); Revenues=2,751,956,000 (FY2009) vs 3,104,183,000 (FY2013)
rationale_evidence.location: Balance sheet / cash flow statement, multi-year comparison

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: A comprehensive 10-K/A (accession 0001193125-12-173208) was filed 2012-04-20, more than 14 months after the original FY2010 10-K (filed 2011-02-25), and this amendment source is used for full income-statement and balance-sheet data points (e.g., CostOfRevenue=763,958,000 FY2009; Liabilities=1,334,532,000 2010-12-31), suggesting a restatement or correction of previously filed financial statements rather than a routine Part III amendment. This pattern is consistent with a prior-period misstatement being corrected, which raises the probability that similar unresolved issues could recur or that comparability of FY2009-FY2010 figures to later periods is impaired.

rationale_evidence.quote: 10-K/A filing_date=2012-04-20 (accession 0001193125-12-173208) restates full financial statement concepts originally reported in the 10-K filed 2011-02-25 (accession 0000950123-11-018066)
rationale_evidence.location: Filing chronology and financial_series_point_in_time accession comparison

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Operating cash flow declined markedly faster (-38%) than net income (-29%) from FY2012 to FY2013 despite stable D&A add-backs, suggesting working-capital or deferred-revenue timing effects that could mask deterioration in underlying cash earnings quality and warrant scrutiny of receivables/payables timing in FY2013.

rationale_evidence.location: Cash flow statement / income statement, FY2012 vs FY2013

top_signals: Comprehensive 10-K/A filed 14+ months after original FY2010 10-K, containing restated full financial-statement line items (accession 0001193125-12-173208)
PP&E net and capex growth (64.6% and 57% respectively, 2009-2013) far outpacing revenue growth (12.8%) and depreciation growth (16%), raising capitalization-policy questions
Operating cash flow deteriorating faster than net income in FY2013 (OCF -38% vs NI -29%), narrowing the cash-to-earnings cushion seen in prior years
Elevated frequency of SEC CORRESP/UPLOAD correspondence clustered in 2012-2014, suggesting active SEC review of filings
Gross margin compression from ~72% (FY2009) to ~69% (FY2013) alongside rising leverage (Liabilities +40% vs Revenues +12.8%)
